title: This Cow Learned to Use a Brush Like a Tool — And She's Super Smart!
summary:
Have you ever seen a cow use a brush to scratch its own back? Probably not — until now! Meet Veronika, a Brown Swiss cow who lives on a small farm in Austria. She's the first cow ever reported to use a tool, and scientists are super excited about it.

Here's what happens: when Veronika feels itchy, she picks up a long-handled deck brush with her tongue. Then she twists her neck to move the brush to exactly the right spot. But that's not even the coolest part. Veronika uses the brush differently depending on where she's scratching. For tough spots like her back, she uses the hard bristles. But for sensitive areas like her belly button or udder, she uses the soft handle instead!

"At the beginning, I thought it was a mistake," said scientist Antonio Osuna-Mascaró. "But after watching her, it was so obvious she was using both ends in different ways."

Scientists used to think only chimpanzees could use the same tool in different ways. But Veronika proves that cows are much smarter than people think. "Most animals are extremely clever if they're given the right task," said another scientist, Lindsay Matthews.

So next time you see a cow, remember — she might be smarter than you think!
why_it_matters: It reminds us that animals we see every day, like cows, may be smarter than we think, so we should treat them with respect.

WHAT: She uses a long-handled deck brush to scratch herself, choosing different ends for different body parts.
WHERE: On a small farm in Austria
WHY: Because she feels itchy and has learned to use the brush as a tool, surprising scientists who thought only chimpanzees could use tools in different ways.
